# Contributing to AWS Secure Landing Zone

Thank you for your interest in contributing! This document explains how to get involved, what we expect, and how to get your changes merged.

## Table of Contents

- [Code of Conduct](#code-of-conduct)
- [Getting Started](#getting-started)
- [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
- [Coding Standards](#coding-standards)
- [Submitting Changes](#submitting-changes)
- [Module Development Guidelines](#module-development-guidelines)
- [Testing](#testing)

---

## Code of Conduct

Be respectful. We follow the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/). Harassment of any kind will not be tolerated.

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

| Tool | Minimum Version | Install |
|------|----------------|---------|
| Terraform | 1.9.0 | [terraform.io](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/install) |
| AWS CLI | 2.x | [aws.amazon.com/cli](https://aws.amazon.com/cli/) |
| pre-commit | 3.0.0 | `pip install pre-commit` |
| tflint | latest | [github.com/terraform-linters/tflint](https://github.com/terraform-linters/tflint) |
| tfsec | latest | [github.com/aquasecurity/tfsec](https://github.com/aquasecurity/tfsec) |
| terraform-docs | 0.16+ | [terraform-docs.io](https://terraform-docs.io) |
| Go | 1.21+ | [go.dev](https://go.dev) (for running tests) |

### Fork and Clone

```bash
# Fork via GitHub UI, then clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/terraform-landing-zone.git
cd terraform-landing-zone

# Add upstream remote to stay in sync
git remote add upstream https://github.com/OluOlus/terraform-landing-zone.git
```

### Install Pre-commit Hooks

```bash
pre-commit install
pre-commit install --hook-type commit-msg
```

This enforces Terraform formatting, security scanning, and conventional commit messages on every commit.

### Run the Fork Setup Script

If you are adapting the landing zone for your own organisation or region, run the interactive setup script first:

```bash
chmod +x scripts/fork-setup.sh
./scripts/fork-setup.sh
```

This replaces naming prefixes, regions, and email placeholders throughout the codebase.

---

## Development Workflow

```
main ← pull requests only (protected branch)
  └── feature/your-feature-name
  └── fix/bug-description
  └── docs/update-readme
```

1. Sync with upstream: `git fetch upstream && git rebase upstream/main`
2. Create a branch: `git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name`
3. Make changes, committing in logical units
4. Push and open a PR against `main`

Commit messages must follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):

```
feat: add S3 Intelligent-Tiering lifecycle rule
fix: correct KMS key policy action name
docs: update deployment guide prerequisites
chore: bump terraform-aws-modules to v5.2
```

---

## Coding Standards

### Terraform

- **Format**: run `terraform fmt -recursive .` before committing (pre-commit handles this automatically)
- **Naming**: use `snake_case` for all resource names, variables, and locals
- **Variables**: every variable needs `description` and `type`; use `validation` blocks for enum-style values
- **Outputs**: every module must have an `outputs.tf`; exported values need `description`
- **Tags**: all resources must accept and apply `var.common_tags`; do not hardcode tag values
- **Regions**: never hardcode AWS regions — use `data.aws_region.current.name` or a validated variable
- **Secrets**: never put credentials, account IDs, or email addresses in `.tf` files; use variables with `sensitive = true` or SSM Parameter Store references
- **KMS**: all S3 buckets, CloudWatch log groups, SNS topics, and Lambda env vars must be encrypted with a customer-managed key
- **Comments**: only add a comment when the *why* is non-obvious — don't narrate what the code does

### Security

- IAM policies must follow least-privilege; avoid `Resource = "*"` for destructive actions
- S3 buckets must have `block_public_acls = true`, versioning enabled, and KMS encryption
- Lambda functions must have `dead_letter_config` for async invocations
- All data sources that filter by region must use `data.aws_region.current.name`, not hardcoded strings

### Documentation

- Each module directory must have a `README.md` generated by `terraform-docs`
- Run `terraform-docs markdown . > README.md` inside a module after changing variables or outputs
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` with every PR under `[Unreleased]`

---

## Submitting Changes

1. Ensure all pre-commit hooks pass: `pre-commit run --all-files`
2. Ensure `terraform validate` passes for all environments you changed
3. Update `CHANGELOG.md` under `[Unreleased]`
4. Open a PR using the pull request template
5. A CODEOWNER will be automatically assigned for review
6. The PR plan workflow will post a Terraform plan output as a comment — review it carefully

### PR Size

Keep PRs small and focused. A PR that changes one module is easier to review than one that touches ten. If your change is large, split it into a logical sequence of smaller PRs.

---

## Module Development Guidelines

When adding a new module:

```
modules/
  your-module/
    main.tf        # Resources
    variables.tf   # All input variables
    outputs.tf     # All output values
    README.md      # Generated by terraform-docs
```

- Variables that are region-specific should validate against the allowed region list
- Add the module to `MODULE_COMPLETION_STATUS.md`
- Add a usage example in a comment block at the top of `main.tf`
- Wire the module into the appropriate environment `main.tf` with a `count` or `for_each` guard so it can be disabled

---

## Testing

### Unit Tests (Go + Terratest)

```bash
cd tests/unit
go test -v -timeout 10m ./...
```

Unit tests validate module logic without deploying real AWS resources.

### Integration Tests

```bash
cd tests/integration
go test -v -timeout 60m ./...
```

Integration tests deploy to a real AWS sandbox account. You need valid AWS credentials with sufficient permissions.

### Validation Scripts

```bash
make lint          # TFLint across all modules
make security-scan # tfsec + Checkov
make test-unit     # Go unit tests
```

---

## Questions?

Open a [GitHub Discussion](https://github.com/OluOlus/terraform-landing-zone/discussions) for questions that aren't bugs or feature requests. For security issues, see [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md).
